objectives: Define core concepts and constraints in embedded systems
Relate digital systems fundamentals to embedded system design decisions
Set up a basic workflow using integrated development environment software for embedded projects
learning_path.title: Embedded Systems Foundations and Digital Systems Overview

objectives: Establish a repeatable development workflow for embedded programming
Apply tools for software configuration management to manage embedded code changes
Organize projects and builds within integrated development environment software
learning_path.title: Programming Workflow and Configuration Management for Embedded Projects

objectives: Explain key architectural elements of Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M) relevant to firmware development
Map architectural features to typical embedded systems use-cases
Create and run a minimal project in integrated development environment software targeting Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M)
learning_path.title: Arm Cortex-M Microcontroller Architecture Essentials

objectives: Differentiate stack and heap usage patterns in embedded systems
Use linker scripts to control memory layout for Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M) targets
Diagnose common memory-related failures in embedded programming
learning_path.title: Memory Management on Embedded Targets: Stack, Heap, and Linker Scripts

objectives: Implement interrupt-driven control flow in embedded systems
Configure and use timers for periodic and time-critical tasks
Analyze latency and timing behavior using Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M) concepts
learning_path.title: Interrupts and Timers on Microcontrollers

objectives: Control digital I/O using Hardware Interfacing (Gpio/Adc/Pwm)
Acquire analog measurements using Hardware Interfacing (Gpio/Adc/Pwm)
Generate PWM signals for actuator-style control in embedded systems
learning_path.title: Hardware Interfacing with GPIO, ADC, and PWM

objectives: Implement basic communication flows using Device Drivers (Spi/I2C/Uart/Can)
Integrate peripheral communication with interrupt-driven designs
Validate data exchange patterns in embedded systems applications
learning_path.title: Device Drivers for SPI, I2C, UART, and CAN

objectives: Apply C++ (Embedded) constructs appropriate for constrained embedded systems
Manage object lifetimes with awareness of Memory Management (Stack/Heap/Linker Scripts)
Structure embedded programming modules for maintainability
learning_path.title: C++ for Embedded Development

objectives: Explain scheduling and task concepts in Rtos (Freertos)
Design task interactions using interrupts and timers appropriately
Integrate device I/O using Device Drivers (Spi/I2C/Uart/Can) within an RTOS-based embedded system
learning_path.title: Real-Time Design with FreeRTOS

objectives: Describe the role of Embedded Linux in embedded systems
Set up a basic Embedded Linux development workflow using integrated development environment software
Compare design tradeoffs between Microcontroller Architecture (Arm Cortex-M) firmware and Embedded Linux approaches
learning_path.title: Embedded Linux Fundamentals and System Bring-Up

objectives: Use automatic programming to generate or automate portions of embedded programming workflows
Integrate automatic programming outputs into tools for software configuration management
Validate generated artifacts within integrated development environment software
learning_path.title: Automatic Programming for Embedded Workflows

objectives: Apply use logic programming to express rules and constraints relevant to embedded systems behavior
Use logic programming to reason about state transitions and event handling involving Interrupts And Timers
Integrate logic-based checks into an embedded programming workflow
learning_path.title: Logic Programming for Embedded Reasoning and Verification Tasks

matched_occupation.description: Embedded systems software developers program, implement, document and maintain software to be run on an embedded system.
matched_occupation.label: embedded systems software developer
